section_name: Meaning of “children are present” in ORS 811.111
chapter_title: Rules of the Rd. for Drivers
text:
For purposes of ORS 811.111 (Violating a speed limit) , children are present at any time and on any day when:

(1) Children are:

(a) Occupying or walking within a crosswalk described in ORS 801.462 (“School zone.”) (1)(b); or

(b) Waiting on the curb or shoulder of the highway at a crosswalk described in ORS 801.462 (“School zone.”) (1)(b); or

(2) A traffic patrol member provided under ORS 339.650 (“Traffic patrol” defined for ORS 339.650 to 339.665) to 339.665 (Intergovernmental cooperation and assistance in connection with traffic patrols) is present to assist children at a crosswalk described in ORS 801.462 (“School zone.”) (1)(b). [1997 c.438 §2; 2003 c.397 §5; 2003 c.819 §§9,9a,9b]
markdown:
## Title 59
### Chapter 811. Rules of the Rd. for Drivers
##### § 811.124 — Meaning of “children are present” in ORS 811.111

For purposes of ORS 811.111 (Violating a speed limit) , children are present at any time and on any day when:

(1) Children are:

    (a) Occupying or walking within a crosswalk described in ORS 801.462 (“School zone.”) (1)(b); or

    (b) Waiting on the curb or shoulder of the highway at a crosswalk described in ORS 801.462 (“School zone.”) (1)(b); or

(2) A traffic patrol member provided under ORS 339.650 (“Traffic patrol” defined for ORS 339.650 to 339.665) to 339.665 (Intergovernmental cooperation and assistance in connection with traffic patrols) is present to assist children at a crosswalk described in ORS 801.462 (“School zone.”) (1)(b). [1997 c.438 §2; 2003 c.397 §5; 2003 c.819 §§9,9a,9b]

[1997 c.438 §2; 2003 c.397 §5; 2003 c.819 §§9,9a,9b]
